title: MRNA vs DNA Viruses
script_summary: A comparison of mRNA and DNA viruses reveals intriguing differences in their function and impact.

segments.voiceover: Here's what science actually says about mRNA vs DNA viruses.
mRNA viruses, like COVID-19, use messenger RNA to hijack our cells, while DNA viruses, such as herpes, utilize DNA.
This difference means mRNA viruses can mutate rapidly, making them elusive targets for our immune system!
Studies show that mRNA vaccines effectively train our immune response against these tricky invaders.
So, while DNA viruses are more stable, mRNA viruses keep us on our toes.
